articles.title: Yellow alert in north Kerala for next 2 days - The Times of India
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Kerala
articles.full_text:
Kochi: IMD has issued a yellow warning in northern districts on Saturday and Sunday. However, no alert has been issued for Wayanad on Saturday.
Meanwhile, the deficiency of rainfall has increased to 34% in
Kerala (June 1 and July 17) with Wayanad reporting highest deficiency figure at 61% and
Idukki with 58%. The number of districts receiving normal rainfall keeps on declining in the state. Currently only Thiruvananthapuram and Kottayam received normal rain from June 1 to July 17 though with deficiencies of 16% and 6%, respectively.
IMD said rain or thundershower is most likely to occur at a most places in the state till July 23. Thunderstorm accompanied by lightning and gusty winds with speed reaching 30-40kmph is very likely to occur at one or two places in Kerala till July 19.
